source.source_rationale: Furthermore, recruitment and activation of type 1 immune cells such as CD8+ T cells, helper T1 (T_(H)1) cells, natural killer (NK) cells and type 1 innate lymphoid cells (ILC1s) contribute to the accumulation of proinflammatory macrophages in the adipose tissue in an interferon (IFN)-g-dependent manner4.

Clustering analysis revealed 19 distinct clusters: adipocyte precursor cells (APCs), preadipocytes (pADs), smooth muscle cells (SMCs), endothelial cells (Endos), conventional type 1 DCs (cDC1s), perivascular macrophages (PVMs), nonclassic monocytes (ncMos), B cells, naive CD4+ T cells, naive CD8+ T cells, cytotoxic CD8+ T cells, CD8+ gd T cells, regulatory T (T_(reg)) cells, mucosa-associated invariant T cells (MAITs), mature NK (mNK) cells, and four previously uncharacterized clusters of myeloid-like cells, conventional type 2B DC-like cells (cDC2Bs), NK-like cells and non-NK ILCs (Extended Data Figs. 1a,b and 2a-c).

However, we also identified the presence of two additional distinct NK cell subsets derived from CD45+Lin^(-)CD7+CD200R1^(-) cells that expressed lower levels of FCGR3A and KLRF1, and displayed similar cluster marker expression with previously identified NK-like cell clusters: one cluster of NK-like cells discerned by expression of IL7R, SELL and CD2 similar to previously described CD56^(bright) immature NK (iNK) cells in human peripheral tissues18, and a population of NK-like cells distinguished by expression of CXCR6, KLRC1 and GZMK consistent with tissue-resident NK (trNK) cells described in the human liver^(19,20) (Fig. 2b and Extended Data Fig. 3b,c).

However, we also identified the presence of two additional distinct NK cell subsets derived from CD45+Lin^(-)CD7+CD200R1^(-) cells that expressed lower levels of FCGR3A and KLRF1, and displayed similar cluster marker expression with previously identified NK-like cell clusters: one cluster of NK-like cells discerned by expression of IL7R, SELL and CD2 similar to previously described CD56^(bright) immature NK (iNK) cells in human peripheral tissues18, and a population of NK-like cells distinguished by expression of CXCR6, KLRC1 and GZMK consistent with tissue-resident NK (trNK) cells described in the human liver^(19,20) (Fig. 2b and Extended Data Fig. 3b,c).

derived.cell_type_label: WAT ILC (white adipose tissue innate lymphoid cell)

source.source_rationale: WAT ILC clusters were defined based on differential expression of marker genes: GATA3 and IL1RL1 consistent with ILC2s described in mice and humans^(21,22,23); ZNF683 and TBX21 consistent with ILC1s described in mice^(16,24); CCR6, IL1R1 and IL23R consistent with ILC3s described in mice and humans^(23,25,26); and SELL and IL1R1 consistent with circulating ILC precursor cells (ILCPs) found in human blood^(27,28) (Fig. 2b,c and Extended Data Fig. 3b,c).

Flow cytometry experiments corroborated the results from our scRNA-seq analysis, supporting the identification of three distinct IFN-g-producing NK cell populations based on PERFORIN, CD62L, CD16, TBET and EOMES expression: TBET^(hi)EOMES^(int)PERFORIN^(hi)CD16+ mNK cells; TBET^(int)EOMES^(hi)PERFORIN^(int)CD16^(-) trNK cells; and TBET^(lo)EOMES^(lo)PERFORIN^(int)CD16^(-)CD62L^(+/-) iNK cells (Fig. 2d,e and Extended Data Fig. 4a,c).

Flow cytometry experiments corroborated the results from our scRNA-seq analysis, supporting the identification of three distinct IFN-g-producing NK cell populations based on PERFORIN, CD62L, CD16, TBET and EOMES expression: TBET^(hi)EOMES^(int)PERFORIN^(hi)CD16+ mNK cells; TBET^(int)EOMES^(hi)PERFORIN^(int)CD16^(-) trNK cells; and TBET^(lo)EOMES^(lo)PERFORIN^(int)CD16^(-)CD62L^(+/-) iNK cells (Fig. 2d,e and Extended Data Fig. 4a,c).

Our analysis identified three populations of monocytes: ncMos based on expression of FCGR3A and HES4, Mo-1s by expression of FCER1A and Mo-2s via increased expression of CSF3R, FCAR and SELL, compared with Mo-1s (Fig. 4b and Extended Data Fig. 6b-d).

Our analysis identified three populations of monocytes: ncMos based on expression of FCGR3A and HES4, Mo-1s by expression of FCER1A and Mo-2s via increased expression of CSF3R, FCAR and SELL, compared with Mo-1s (Fig. 4b and Extended Data Fig. 6b-d).

Our analysis identified three populations of monocytes: ncMos based on expression of FCGR3A and HES4, Mo-1s by expression of FCER1A and Mo-2s via increased expression of CSF3R, FCAR and SELL, compared with Mo-1s (Fig. 4b and Extended Data Fig. 6b-d).

The scRNA-seq analysis also identified a population of human WAT neutrophils based on expression of THBS1 and S100A12, which displayed enrichment for the neutrophil gene module compared with other myeloid cell clusters (Fig. 4b and Extended Data Fig. 6b,c,f).

The scRNA-seq analysis also identified a population of human WAT neutrophils based on expression of THBS1 and S100A12, which displayed enrichment for the neutrophil gene module compared with other myeloid cell clusters (Fig. 4b and Extended Data Fig. 6b,c,f).

We confirmed the presence of two previously identified macrophage subsets present within the human WAT: TREM2, CD9 and LPL expressing lipid-associated macrophages (LAMs)8, LYVE1, SELENOP and C1Q, expressing PVMs32, and an uncharacterized inflammatory macrophage (IM) population with high expression of CCL3L1, TNF and CXCL3 (Fig. 4b and Extended Data Fig. 6b,c).

In contrast to our CD45+-sorted scRNA-seq dataset, DC-sorted scRNA-seq analysis identified three distinct WAT DC populations: conventional type 1 DCs (cDC1s) defined by expression of IRF8, DPP4, CADM1 and XCR1, coventional type 2 DCs subset A (cDC2As), via expression of CD1C, IRF4, IL7R and LAMP3, and conventional type 2 DCs subset B (cDC2Bs) by increased expression of CD1C, IRF4, FCER1A and CLEC10A, consistent with the phenotypes of DC subsets recently reported in human blood34 (Fig. 4f, Extended Data Fig. 6b,c and Supplementary Table 4).

In contrast to our CD45+-sorted scRNA-seq dataset, DC-sorted scRNA-seq analysis identified three distinct WAT DC populations: conventional type 1 DCs (cDC1s) defined by expression of IRF8, DPP4, CADM1 and XCR1, coventional type 2 DCs subset A (cDC2As), via expression of CD1C, IRF4, IL7R and LAMP3, and conventional type 2 DCs subset B (cDC2Bs) by increased expression of CD1C, IRF4, FCER1A and CLEC10A, consistent with the phenotypes of DC subsets recently reported in human blood34 (Fig. 4f, Extended Data Fig. 6b,c and Supplementary Table 4).

derived.cell_type_label: WAT cDC1s (white adipose tissue conventional type 1 dendritic cells)

source.source_rationale: WAT cDC1s were distinguished by expression of DPP4 (CD26) CD26+CD1C^(-), whereas cDC2s could be differentiated into two subsets by expression of CD206, CD14, FCER1A and CLEC10A: cDC2Bs (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206+CD14^(hi)FCER1A+CLEC10A+); cDC2As (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206-CD14^(int)FCER1A^(-)CLEC10A^(-)) (Fig. 4g and Extended Data Fig. 7d).

derived.cell_type_label: WAT cDC2s (white adipose tissue conventional type 2 dendritic cells)

source.source_rationale: WAT cDC1s were distinguished by expression of DPP4 (CD26) CD26+CD1C^(-), whereas cDC2s could be differentiated into two subsets by expression of CD206, CD14, FCER1A and CLEC10A: cDC2Bs (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206+CD14^(hi)FCER1A+CLEC10A+); cDC2As (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206-CD14^(int)FCER1A^(-)CLEC10A^(-)) (Fig. 4g and Extended Data Fig. 7d).

derived.cell_type_label: WAT cDC2s (white adipose tissue conventional type 2 dendritic cells)

source.source_rationale: WAT cDC1s were distinguished by expression of DPP4 (CD26) CD26+CD1C^(-), whereas cDC2s could be differentiated into two subsets by expression of CD206, CD14, FCER1A and CLEC10A: cDC2Bs (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206+CD14^(hi)FCER1A+CLEC10A+); cDC2As (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206-CD14^(int)FCER1A^(-)CLEC10A^(-)) (Fig. 4g and Extended Data Fig. 7d).

derived.cell_type_label: WAT cDC2s (white adipose tissue conventional type 2 dendritic cells)

source.source_rationale: WAT cDC1s were distinguished by expression of DPP4 (CD26) CD26+CD1C^(-), whereas cDC2s could be differentiated into two subsets by expression of CD206, CD14, FCER1A and CLEC10A: cDC2Bs (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206+CD14^(hi)FCER1A+CLEC10A+); cDC2As (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206-CD14^(int)FCER1A^(-)CLEC10A^(-)) (Fig. 4g and Extended Data Fig. 7d).

derived.cell_type_label: WAT cDC2s (white adipose tissue conventional type 2 dendritic cells)

source.source_rationale: WAT cDC1s were distinguished by expression of DPP4 (CD26) CD26+CD1C^(-), whereas cDC2s could be differentiated into two subsets by expression of CD206, CD14, FCER1A and CLEC10A: cDC2Bs (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206+CD14^(hi)FCER1A+CLEC10A+); cDC2As (CD26^(-)CD1C+CD206-CD14^(int)FCER1A^(-)CLEC10A^(-)) (Fig. 4g and Extended Data Fig. 7d).

derived.cell_type_label: conventional type 2 dendritic cells subset A (cDC2As)

source.source_rationale: Furthermore, cDC2As uniquely expressed the chemokines CCL17, CCL22 and CCL19, and the cytokine IL-15, which interacted with ILCs, NK cells and APCs, suggesting additional roles for cDC2As in the recruitment and maintenance of innate lymphocytes and progenitor cell types in the lean WAT.

derived.cell_type_label: conventional type 2 dendritic cells subset A (cDC2As)

source.source_rationale: Furthermore, cDC2As uniquely expressed the chemokines CCL17, CCL22 and CCL19, and the cytokine IL-15, which interacted with ILCs, NK cells and APCs, suggesting additional roles for cDC2As in the recruitment and maintenance of innate lymphocytes and progenitor cell types in the lean WAT.

derived.cell_type_label: conventional type 2 dendritic cells subset A (cDC2As)

source.source_rationale: Furthermore, cDC2As uniquely expressed the chemokines CCL17, CCL22 and CCL19, and the cytokine IL-15, which interacted with ILCs, NK cells and APCs, suggesting additional roles for cDC2As in the recruitment and maintenance of innate lymphocytes and progenitor cell types in the lean WAT.

derived.cell_type_label: conventional type 2 dendritic cells subset A (cDC2As)

source.source_rationale: Furthermore, cDC2As uniquely expressed the chemokines CCL17, CCL22 and CCL19, and the cytokine IL-15, which interacted with ILCs, NK cells and APCs, suggesting additional roles for cDC2As in the recruitment and maintenance of innate lymphocytes and progenitor cell types in the lean WAT.

derived.cell_type_label: conventional type 2 dendritic cells subset B (cDC2Bs)

source.source_rationale: cDC2Bs expressed MIF, IL-18, IL-1b and TNFSF9, interacting with ILCs, NK cells and myeloid cells, suggesting that DC populations may exacerbate adipose tissue inflammation and tissue fibrosis in a similar manner to proinflammatory macrophages.
